description: Curated tactical test positions from 3000+ Elo human games. Each sample is a 5-6 move window with a clear right/wrong answer judged by game outcome.
notation: chess.com 4PC (e.g., h2-h3, Ne1-f3, Qg1xj4). Convert to Valhalla long-algebraic (e.g., h2h3, e1f3, g1j4) before feeding to engine.

samples.name: Four-Queen Pileup
samples.moves_to_replay: g2g4 b7c7 g13g12 m6l6 f2f3 b9c9 h14f12 m7k7 k2k4 b4d4 i13i11 n10l9 e1d3 a4c4 f12f6 n7m6 d3e5 a5c6 d13d11 n5l4 e5c4 b5c4 j14i12 m11k11 g1g3 a8b7 e14f12 m6k8 j2j3 a10c11 d14d12 l4n5 g3i5 c11b9 d12e12 k8m6 f1i4 b9d8 i12h10 n5m7 j1i3 b8c8 f6h8 l9j8 i5i9 c7d7 e12e9 m7l9 i3g2 a9b8 h13h11 m4l4 f3f4 c6b4 h8h5 m10l10 i1j2 b4c6 k13k12 n6k9 i9i5 d8b9 i14h13 n4n5 h1j1 b7c7 f14h12 n9m10 i5g7 c7b7 e9e10 h1n8 g7f6 a11a10 h5g6 m6k8
samples.position_after: After R19 (all 4 moves). Red is about to play R20.
samples.consequence: Triggers 3 more queen captures. Red loses queen but gains knight. Yellow survives with queen on c6. Chain favors Yellow.
samples.what_to_check: Does engine see the full capture chain? Does it evaluate whether starting the chain is worth it (Red loses queen for knight = -600cp)?

samples.name: Three-Way Exchange
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b7c7 g13g12 m8l8 e1f3 a8b7 j14i12 m6l6 g1h2 b11d11 h14e11 n10l9 e2e4 b4c4 j13j12 m11l11 j1i3 a10c9 k13k11 m5k5 k2k3 a5c6 i14j13 n9m8 h2f4 b10c10 e14f12 n7m6 i1h2 b7d9 h14i14 m8n7 j2j3 d9d8 f14h12 m4k4 j3j4 d8d7 d13d12 k5j4 f4j4 a9b10 e11g9 m7l7 f1e2 c9e8 g9h10 n11n10 h14i14 b10e7 h10g10 n6m7 j4g4
samples.position_after: After Red plays Qj4-g4 in R14. Blue is about to respond.
samples.consequence: Blue captures Red's queen (+900cp). Yellow then captures knight Qg10xNe8 (+300cp). Blue won the game with 87pts. This is THE pivotal capture.

samples.name: Three-Round Explosion
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b9c9 g13g12 m8l8 g1j4 a8b9 i13i12 n7m8 f2f4 b7c7 d13d11 m4k4
samples.position_after: After R3 complete. Red's queen is on j4, Yellow's queen on h14, Green's queen on m8.
samples.consequence: Queen enters center. Yellow responds Qh14xm9+ capturing Green. This triggers a 3-round chain that eliminates Green and redistributes material.

samples.name: Knight Wins Rook
samples.moves_to_replay: g2g4 b7c7 g13g12 m6l6 f2f3 b9c9 h14f12 m7k7 k2k4 b4d4 i13i11 n10l9 e1d3 a4c4 f12f6 n7m6
samples.position_after: After R4 complete. Red's knight is on d3, Blue's rook is on c4.
samples.consequence: Knight advances to e5 attacking Blue's rook on c4. Next move Ne5xRc4 wins the rook. Net +200cp for Red.

samples.name: Queen Hangs - Free Material
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b7c7 g13g12 m8l8 e1f3 a8b7 j14i12 m6l6 g1h2 b11d11 h14e11 n10l9 e2e4 b4c4 j13j12 m11l11 j1i3 a10c9 k13k11 m5k5 k2k3 a5c6 i14j13 n9m8 h2f4 b10c10 e14f12 n7m6 i1h2 b7d9 h14i14 m8n7 j2j3 d9d8 f14h12 m4k4 j3j4 d8d7 d13d12 k5j4 f4j4 a9b10 e11g9 m7l7 f1e2 c9e8 g9h10 n11n10 h14i14 b10e7 h10g10 n6m7
samples.position_after: After R13 complete. Red is about to move.
samples.consequence: Red (3438 Elo!) moves queen to g4 where Blue can capture it. Even the best players blunder.
samples.what_to_check: Does engine see that g4 is attacked by Blue's queen on d7? Engine MUST NOT play this move.

samples.name: Bishop Sacrifice Cracks King
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b9c9 d13d11 m8l8 f2f4 b4d4 i13i12 m11k11 e1f3 a5c6 h13h11 n7k10 g1j4 b8d8 h14h12 n9m8 j1i3 b5c5 e14f12 n10l9 j4h6 b9d7 k12i11 k10k8 f3g5 c9c11 f12g10 l9k7 k2k4 a10c11 g10i9 j7i9
samples.position_after: After R10 complete. Green's bishop is on m8, Red's h-pawn is on h3.
samples.consequence: Bishop captures h3, cracking open Red's king shelter. Red never castles. Checkmated R21 with 9pts.

samples.name: Queen Invasion to Checkmate
samples.position_after: After R17 complete. Blue's queen is on k7. Red's king shelter is cracked.
samples.consequence: Queen enters Red's territory. Next: QxBg3, Qh2+. Delivers checkmate R21.

samples.name: Promotion into Checkmate
samples.position_after: After R23. Yellow's k-pawn is on k8. Green's king is walking (DKW since R5).
samples.consequence: Pawn promotes to queen WITH CHECK. Next move: Qk7-j7# checkmate.

samples.name: Castle During Chaos
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b4d4 i13i12 m11k11 g1j4 b9c9 h13h11 m6l6 k2k4 b8d8 d13d11 m7k7 f2f4 a5c6 e14f12 n10l9 j1i3 b11c11 j14k12 n6k9 j4i5 a9c7 g13g11 k9f14 i5m5 a8b8 d14f14 n5m7 e1d3 b5c5 g11g10 m8l8 m5l6 a6d3 k12l10 n11n10 l6m7 d3k10 l10n9 l9m7 i3j5 k10n7 n9m7 n4n7
samples.position_after: After R15 complete. Red has material lead. King still on h1.
samples.consequence: Red castles while opponents are fighting. Red survives, promotes h-pawn R31, wins with 79pts.

samples.name: Castle R10 - Winner's Move
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b9c9 d13d11 m8l8 f2f4 b4d4 i13i12 m11k11 e1f3 a5c6 h13h11 n7k10 g1j4 b8d8 h14h12 n9m8 j1i3 b5c5 e14f12 n10l9 j4h6 b9d7 k12i11 k10k8 f3g5 c9c11 f12g10 l9k7 k2k4 a10c11 g10i9 j7i9 h6h10 a9b5 d14d12 j10i9
samples.position_after: After R9 complete. Blue has both knights out, bishop developed, queen active.
samples.consequence: Blue castles while 3 opponents are in tactical skirmishes. Blue won with 70pts. Every winner castled.

samples.name: Queen Sacrifice Enables Mate
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b9c9 i13i11 m11k11 k2k4 b8d8 d13d11 m6l6 f2f4 b4d4 j14i12 m7k7 e2e4 a5c6 j13j11 n5l4 f1e2 a8b9 i11i10 n7l7 e2g4 b9b8 e14d12 n6m7 g1i3 b8d6 h14i13 m8l8
samples.position_after: After R7 complete. Green's queen is on l7, Red's pawn is on h3.
samples.test_move: Sequence test: R8 Green plays Ql7xh3+, then R9 Blue plays Qf4-f1+#
samples.consequence: Green sacrifices queen on h3 with check. Red MUST recapture. This opens f1 for Blue's queen checkmate.

samples.name: Promotion Immediately Captured
samples.position_after: Blue's e-pawn is on e7, ready to promote.
samples.consequence: Blue promotes. Yellow immediately captures. Blue recaptures with rook. Net: rook deep on e8.

samples.name: Bishop Cascade into Checkmate
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b7c7 i13i11 m11k11 e1f3 b4d4 j14i12 n10l9 g1h2 b9d9 d13d11 m8l8 e2e4 a8c6 h13h12 l9k7 k2k4 a10c9 i14g12 m10l10 j1i3 d9e9 k13k12 n9m8
samples.position_after: After R6 complete. Multiple bishop exchanges about to happen.
samples.consequence: Bishop exchanges cascade. By R9, Blue checkmates Yellow. Red stays OUT of the chain, wins with 60pts.

samples.name: Red Cleanup After Chain
samples.moves_to_replay_note: Full R1-R9 of game 93333795 needed. Yellow is checkmated.
samples.position_after: After R9 complete. Yellow is dead. Red has been developing while others fought.
samples.consequence: Red starts collecting material from weakened opponents. Won with 60pts.

samples.name: Pawn March to Promotion
samples.position_after: Red's e-pawn is on e4. Path to e8 is clearable.
samples.consequence: Red pushes e-pawn from e4 to promotion in 6 rounds. Red won with 60pts.

samples.name: Bishop Checkmate Finish
samples.position_after: Red's bishop is on d3, opponent's king is exposed.
samples.consequence: Bishop delivers checkmate. Red wins with 60pts.

samples.name: Gangster_H Signature Opening
samples.position_after: After R1. All 4 players played one pawn move.
samples.consequence: Queen out R2. Won 2 of 3 games with this opening.

samples.name: Queen Overextension - Fatal
samples.position_after: After R11 complete. Red's queen is on g9. Green just cracked Red's king shelter.
samples.consequence: Red captures a pawn with check instead of defending. Queen stranded at m9. Checkmated R21 with 9pts.

samples.name: King March to Death
samples.position_after: Red's king is on h1, not castled, pawns pushed forward.
samples.consequence: King walks. Checkmated R17 with 15pts. King movement without shelter = death in 4PC.

samples.name: Pawn Spam Opening
samples.position_after: Starting position.
samples.test_move: Does engine play 5+ pawn moves in first 6?
samples.consequence: Queen never activated. Checkmated R14 with 3pts.

samples.name: Deep Queen Raid - Aggression
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h3 b4d4 i13i12 m11k11 g1j4 b9c9 h13h11 m6l6 k2k4 b8d8 d13d11 m7k7 f2f4 a5c6 e14f12 n10l9 j1i3 b11c11 j14k12 n6k9 j4i5 a9c7 g13g11 k9f14
samples.position_after: After R6 complete. Red's queen is on i5. Green's pawn on m5 is undefended.
samples.consequence: Queen captures deep in Green's territory. Three captures in 4 moves = +700cp. Red won with 79pts.

samples.name: Early Queen Activation - Yellow R2
samples.position_after: After R1 complete.
samples.consequence: Queen active round 2. Yellow tied for first with 73pts.

samples.name: Rook Lift R8
samples.moves_to_replay: g2g4 b7c7 g13g12 m6l6 f2f3 b9c9 h14f12 m7k7 k2k4 b4d4 i13i11 n10l9 e1d3 a4c4 f12f6 n7m6 d3e5 a5c6 d13d11 n5l4 e5c4 b5c4 j14i12 m11k11 g1g3 a8b7 e14f12 m6k8
samples.position_after: After R7 complete. Yellow's rook is on d14.
samples.consequence: Rook lift to d12, then d12-e12. Tied for 1st with 73pts.

samples.name: Patient Development - Green Wins
samples.moves_to_replay: h2h4 b7c7 g13g12 m11k11 g1h2 a8b7 h14g13 m6l6 e1f3 a10c9 j14i12 n10l9
samples.position_after: After R3 complete. Green has one knight on l9.
samples.consequence: Both knights out by R4. Green won with 64pts.

samples.name: Endgame King Hunt
samples.position_after: Blue has king + bishop + knight. Green's rook is hunting.
samples.consequence: Green's rook captures pawn and knight while checking Blue's king.
